novel_features.name: Recall check by drug
novel_features.description: Find active FDA recalls that mention a drug, optionally filtered to the most serious class.
novel_features.rationale: Wraps the openFDA enforcement search with the safety contract: no match reports 'no recall records found', never that the drug is safe.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs the recall status of a specific medication by name.

novel_features.name: Recalls by firm
novel_features.description: List every recall attributed to a recalling firm or manufacturer.
novel_features.rationale: Pre-builds the recalling_firm search so an agent does not hand-roll openFDA Lucene syntax.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to audit a manufacturer's recall history.

novel_features.name: Recent recalls window
novel_features.description: List recalls initiated in the last N days, most recent first.
novel_features.rationale: Computes the inclusive date range and openFDA date-range query for a rolling window.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for a periodic sweep of newly initiated drug recalls.

novel_features.name: Recall detail by number
novel_features.description: Show full detail for a single recall number, with the FDA class legend.
novel_features.rationale: Exact recall_number lookup with the class meanings printed in place for interpretation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to expand one recall's full facts after a check/firm/recent lookup surfaces its number.

narrative.headline: Search FDA drug recall records by drug, firm, recency, or recall number — keyless, with a safety contract that never calls a drug "safe".
narrative.value_prop: A keyless CLI over the openFDA drug enforcement endpoint. The check, firm, recent, and reference commands wrap FDA recall search with pre-built queries; every result cites the recall number, prints the FDA class legend, and carries an enforcement-not-medical-advice disclaimer. A drug with no recall is reported as 'no recall records found', never as safe.

quickstart.comment: confirm the CLI and openFDA endpoint are reachable
narrow to the most serious (Class I) recalls
recalls initiated in the last 30 days

troubleshoots.fix: This means no FDA enforcement record matched — it is not a statement that the drug is safe. Consult a pharmacist or doctor.
openFDA is rate limited to 240 requests/minute per IP; wait and retry.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI to look up FDA drug recall (enforcement) facts: which drugs or manufacturers have active or historical recalls, the recall class, reason, and distribution. It reports FDA enforcement data only.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to decide whether a medication is safe to take — it reports recalls, not safety.
Do not use it for drug interactions, dosing, or clinical advice.
Do not use it for device or food recalls; it covers drug enforcement only.

recipes.title: Serious recalls for a drug
recipes.explanation: Class I recalls for a drug, narrowed to the recall number and reason.

recipes.title: Manufacturer recall history
recipes.explanation: Every recall attributed to a firm, as JSON.

recipes.title: Rolling 7-day sweep
recipes.explanation: Recalls initiated in the last week, most recent first.
